task_completion.description: Download and load the 2SOD PDB structure in PyMOL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL application is open with '2SOD' visible in the object list (right panel) or the command line history showing a 'fetch 2sod' command.

task_completion.description: Generate an electrostatic surface potential visualization of the protein.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL 3D viewer displays a surface representation of the protein colored by electrostatic potential (typically a red-white-blue gradient indicating negative, neutral, and positive regions).

task_completion.description: Render and save the visualization as a high-resolution PNG figure to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file explorer, or PyMOL command line showing the image being saved to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/sod1_electrostatic.png' (e.g., using the 'png' command).

task_completion.description: Create the text report file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation or editing of '~/PyMOL_Data/sod1_electrostatic_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the required lists of charged residues and describe the charge distribution pattern guiding the substrate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains lists of positive and negative residues, and includes descriptive text about the electrostatic funnel/channel guiding the substrate (look for keywords like funnel, channel, guidance, electrostatic, positive, attract).

task_completion.description: Identify the key arginine residue critical for substrate guidance in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report explicitly names Arg143 (or Arginine 143) as the key residue for guidance.

integrity.description: The agent actually used PyMOL to generate the electrostatic surface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the PyMOL interface with the APBS plugin, vacuum electrostatics action, or relevant PyMOL commands being executed, rather than just downloading a pre-made image.

integrity.description: The saved image corresponds to the visualization created in PyMOL.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final rendered image should visually match the 3D structure and coloring seen in the PyMOL viewer during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent wrote the report based on analysis rather than copying a pre-existing solution file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing, echoing, or formatting the text into the report file in a text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing bovine SOD1 (PDB: 2SOD), but the scoring logic strictly expects human SOD1 residue numbering. The key arginine residue MUST be identified as Arg143 in the report (even though in 2SOD it is actually Arg141). The report must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/sod1_electrostatic_report.txt and should contain the conceptual keywords: funnel, channel, guidance, electrostatic, positive, attract. The figure must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/sod1_electrostatic.png.
